sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_array (id UInt32, numbers Array(Int32));
SELECT id, arrayElementOrNull(numbers, 2) AS second_element FROM test_array WHERE arrayElementOrNull(numbers, id) = 2;
SELECT id, arrayElementOrNull(numbers, -1) AS last_element FROM test_array WHERE arrayElementOrNull(numbers, -2) = 5;
SELECT id FROM test_array WHERE arrayElementOrNull(numbers, arrayElementOrNull(numbers, 3)) IS NULL;
SELECT id, SUM(arrayElementOrNull(numbers, number_id)) AS sum_element FROM (SELECT id, arrayElementOrNull(numbers, id) AS number_id FROM test_array) GROUP BY id HAVING sum_element > 5;
CREATE TABLE test_join (join_id UInt32, ref_array Array(Int32));
SELECT a.id, a.numbers, b.ref_array FROM test_array AS a JOIN test_join AS b ON arrayElementOrNull(a.numbers, 1) = arrayElementOrNull(b.ref_array, 1);
SELECT a.id, COUNT(*) AS count_elements FROM test_array AS a WHERE arrayElementOrNull(a.numbers, 1) = arrayElementOrNull(a.numbers, -1) GROUP BY a.id HAVING count_elements > 0;
